turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Booking or Changing a medical appointment 🗨️

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Hi, I’d like to make an appointment to see a doctor.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course. What’s the reason for your visit today?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, my name is Pamela, and I want to make an appointment with the doctor, because I've had a sore throat and cough for a few days. And cough for a few days.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, Pamela. Sorry you’re not feeling well. How long exactly have you had these symptoms?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe 5 or 7 days.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. Have you had any fever or other symptoms besides the sore throat and cough?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, I have a pain in my nose too.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, thanks for letting me know. Would you prefer to see a male or female doctor for your appointment?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: For me, it's okay, whatever. It's not a problem for me.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright. Do you have a preferred day or time for the appointment?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Do you have appointment on Saturday?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Let me check—yes, we do have Saturday slots. Would you prefer morning or afternoon?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I prefer morning, please.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure thing. We have openings at  a.m. and  a.m.—which time works better for you?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: At 9.30, it's OK, please.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect. Can I confirm your phone number, Pamela, in case we need to reach you about your appointment?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, for sure, it's 06-412-5152-63. And do I need to bring my Medicare card?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks, I’ve noted your number. Yes, please bring your Medicare card and a photo ID when you come in. Would you like me to send you a text reminder for your appointment?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please. It will be fine. How long is the appointment likely to take?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Usually about 15 to 20 minutes, depending on what the doctor needs to check. Will this be your first visit to our clinic, Pamela?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: This is my first visit, so do I need to bring something else with me?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good question. Just your Medicare card, photo ID, and any medications you’re currently taking. Do you have any allergies to medicines that the doctor should know about?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Not really, I'm not allergic to medicines, so it's okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, that’s good to know. You’re all set for Saturday at  a.m. Would you like directions to the clinic as well?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please. And do you have parking for patients?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, we do—there’s free parking just behind the clinic. We’re located at 25 Greenfield Road, right next to the pharmacy. Will you be driving yourself here?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, of course, and what is the name of my doctor?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Your appointment will be with Dr. Sarah Collins, one of our general practitioners. Would you like me to note if you prefer to see her again in the future?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I want to know how I feel in the appointment, so after that I will tell you if I want to see her again in the future.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That sounds perfectly fine, Pamela. You can decide after your visit. Is there anything else you’d like to ask about your appointment?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, I have another question. In fact, do you think that I need a plot test for this type of appointment?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Just to be sure—did you mean a swab test or blood test?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, yeah, you're right. Swap test.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it! For a sore throat and cough, the doctor might do a throat swab if she suspects an infection, but it depends on what she finds during the exam. Have you taken any medicine so far for your symptoms?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, for relief my pain, three days ago I went to chemist, and they give me demazine. There are tablets, two days tablets during the day and two tablets during the night.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, thanks for explaining. Has the Demazine helped at all with your sore throat or cough?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No, not really. That's the reason that I prefer go to the doctor.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Makes sense, Pamela. It’s best to have it checked properly. Just to confirm—are you still coughing a lot, or has it gotten a bit better?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, a little bit. I can say that my cough has gotten a bit better.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s good to hear. Since you’re improving slightly, the doctor will likely check your throat and chest to see if you need any extra treatment. Is there anything else you’d like to ask before your visit on Saturday?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Is bulk billing available for students?And do you offer a kind of language support for appointments? Or can I bring a person with me, a friend, that can help me with the translation?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, we do bulk bill students if they have a valid Medicare card, so you’ll be covered. And sure—you can bring a friend to help with translation, that’s no problem at all. Would you like me to note that in your booking?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please. It will be fine. And the other thing, the book bill, it's fantastic. Thank you very much.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re very welcome, Pamela. I’ve added the note about your friend and confirmed bulk billing for you. See you on Saturday at  a.m.—take care until then!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Thank you, see you, bye.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re welcome, Pamela. See you on Saturday—take care and feel better soon. Bye! ____________
